  • invoices, menu (business databases)
  • webserver, vhserver (infrastructure)

You're scared to put numbers in your cold email because you think it's a promise

It's not

Use these:
→ "On average"
→ "Up to"
→ "Our clients typically"

Everyone's still asking which AI model is smartest.

That was the right question in 2024. In 2026 it's almost irrelevant.

The teams winning aren't chasing model updates. They're building workflows that work with any model.

Models get faster every 3 months. Workflows compound.

95% of people who sign up for an AI automation tool never deploy a single workflow in production.

i was one of them for 3 months.

signed up for every tool. watched every tutorial. bookmarked every thread. built zero workflows that actually ran for a client.

then i changed one

Here's the exact approach I used to finalize my SaaS idea.

I call it the validated market strategy:

→ Find a product already doing $1M+ ARR
→ Read every 1-star and 2-star review you can find
→ Look for the same complaint repeated 50+ times
→ Build specifically for that
